title: Meet the teenager tasked with filling Alexia Putellas’ boots at Barcelona
description: “They’re the best in the world; they prove it every year by playing so well. I mean … we play very well,” Clara Serrajordi said with a laugh after reaching the Champions League final at Camp Nou last May, beating Bayern Munich in last season’s semi-finals. The 18-year-old midfielder’s confusion with “they” and “we” makes sense. Until last season, she was on the outside looking in. Serrajordi was used to watching the first team from her home at La Masia, the famed Barcelona youth academy known fo
fullText:
“They’re the best in the world; they prove it every year by playing so well. I mean … we play very well,” Clara Serrajordi said with a laugh after reaching the Champions League final at Camp Nou last May, beating Bayern Munich in last season’s semi-finals.

The 18-year-old midfielder’s confusion with “they” and “we” makes sense. Until last season, she was on the outside looking in.

Serrajordi was used to watching the first team from her home at La Masia, the famed Barcelona youth academy known for fostering top talent in the club’s pipeline. The team’s six previous Champions League final appearances were games she only saw from afar. Less than a decade ago, she waited anxiously in the stands to see if the team’s all-time leading goalscorer, Alexia Putellas, would take a photo with her.

But in the span of a year, her daydreams became a reality.

She has gone from taking photos with Putellas to forming the starting midfield trio alongside her and Patri Guijarro — both of whom she calls her big sisters. Together they won Liga F, Copa de la Reina, the Spanish Super Cup and the Champions League.

Next season, she will face yet another tall task, becoming her idol’s successor after Putellas’ recent move to England’s London City Lionesses. It requires filling the shoes of a player who spent 14 years at Barcelona, won 38 trophies and is the club’s joint-second highest all-time scorer, alongside Cesar Rodriguez on 232 goals, trailing only Lionel Messi (672).

Barcelona knew players like Putellas, Mapi Leon and Ona Batlle would leave and wanted to protect itself from yet another offseason of departures. However, without the financial resources to sign players without restraint, Barcelona are limited by an economic crisis affecting the entire club.

Instead, they looked to players coming through from La Masia. Players like Serrajordi.

Serrajordi passed her first test, replacing Bonmati, the current Ballon d’Or holder, after she suffered a transverse fracture of her left fibula in November and was out until May. The youngster stepped up for her club and made her debut for Spain’s senior national team aged 17.

In the second leg of the Champions League semi-finals, she controlled both legs against Bayern Munich from the centre of midfield. After that 5-3 aggregate victory, at the age of 18 and 167 days, she became the youngest Barcelona player to start a Champions League final against OL Lyonnes in Oslo.

She played 42 matches in her first season with the senior team, starting 25, scoring twice and providing two assists. Her achievements make her a clear contender to succeed team-mate Vicky Lopez as the next Golden Girl, the annual award given to the best female under-21 football player in Europe.

Some within the club describe Serrajordi as a cross between Putellas and Guijarro. They even joke that she looks like a combination of the two. Though she started as an attacking midfielder, in recent years in Barcelona’s youth teams, she has played as a defensive midfielder.

Described as having a powerful physique, keen ball-winning skills and an ability to anticipate the next play. She possesses technical ability and excellent dribbling skills to break through opposition lines.

The tactical astuteness is not limited to the pitch. As a child, she used to compete in chess tournaments, but eventually gave it up due to the commitment. She even played chess against Barcelona men’s player Pedri for a commercial. The two share an agency, Leaderbrock Sports Group. Her father, upon seeing the pictures of the game, asked, “Did you beat Pedri at least?”

Despite still being in her teens, Serrajordi rarely checks social media to see what people are saying about her. Instead, she maintains her calm, taking everything that’s happened over the last year in her stride.

Others might have been overwhelmed by such a daunting challenge, but Serrajordi approached it with the positive, carefree attitude of a younger generation, similar to Lamine Yamal, Lopez or Pau Cubarsi.

When Serrajordi returned to Spain after winning her first Champions League title, she went back to La Masia, where she will continue to live until November of this year. However, replacing a player she looked up to is not just difficult on the pitch, but challenging in other ways.

When Putellas announced her departure, Serrajordi was one of the most emotional players at the farewell ceremony, despite having played alongside her for just one season.

“I would have liked to have made it (into the first team) sooner,” Serrajordi said in an interview with Diari Ara in May. “But I think she’s done everything she needed to do at Barça and has opened many doors for us.”

During the Champions League celebrations, before La Reina’s departure was public, Putellas was very active in supporting Serrajordi, including featuring her in a post-game, pre-party Instagram Live.

The day after returning from Oslo, Norway, Putellas used her time on the microphone during the public celebrations at Montjuïc to chant, “Serra, Serra.”

“We baked the cake and now they’ll eat it,” Putellas said, looking at her younger team-mates during her farewell.

“Becoming a new Alexia is impossible for me. There will never be anyone like her. That much is clear, and I don’t put that pressure on myself because I won’t be her,” Serrajordi said in an interview with Diari Ara.

Still, with her presence, the future of Barcelona’s midfield remains in safe hands.

This article originally appeared in The Athletic.

2026 The Athletic Media Company

title: What Stephen A. Smith told the ESPN ‘bosses’ after learning of Ryan Clark’s firing
description: Ryan Clark’s departure from ESPN on Monday has seen numerous big players in the sports media industry share their thoughts on the development. It is believed ESPN have made a number of cost-cutting firings ahead of the new 2026 NFL season getting underway in just a matter of weeks.
fullText:
Ryan Clark’s departure from ESPN on Monday has seen numerous big players in the sports media industry share their thoughts on the development.

It is believed ESPN have made a number of cost-cutting firings ahead of the new 2026 NFL season getting underway in just a matter of weeks.

Clark has been a regular on ESPN having joined the network all the way back in 2015 following a successful career as a safety in the NFL.

However, the nature of Clark’s departure is what has really frustrated some of his colleagues as he was informed of the decision midway through an NFL Live show on ESPN.

It has been suggested that Clark’s confrontational manner contributed to his ESPN firing following a heated exchange with Peter Schrager last year.

However, established talking head Stephen A. Smith has now weighed in on the matter to share his frustrations with the ESPN chiefs.

Clark is just one of 10 members of the ESPN broadcasting team that has been laid off since the start of the week as the cost-cutting exercise takes effect.

Smith has established himself as one of the top voices on ESPN, which is why his latest comments on Clark’s firing will hold some value.

“A person I considered to be the best NFL analyst in America, is no longer a part of ESPN. I can’t even tell y’all how much that hurt,” said Smith on his YouTube channel.

“I’m gonna miss him. I’m not happy with this decision. The bosses know I’m not happy. I made this very clear.”

The departures from ESPN are expected to continue over the coming days as their cutbacks provide some shocking layoffs.

It has even been suggested that NFL Insider Tom Pelissero is one of the names who could follow Clark out of the doors at ESPN.

There are some seriously established figures in the American sports broadcasting business who are heading into the summer as free agents.

Someone like Pelissero would unlikely be out of work for too long, but it does go to show the extent to which ESPN are going to in order to cut back on their costs.

Why Seattle Seahawks new owner could save billions of dollars in taxes with $9.6bn takeover

Roger Goodell’s $64m NFL salary to explode as masterplan for 18-game season accelerates

Why Pat McAfee ‘loves’ NFL players calling for grass pitches to not be ripped up after World Cup

title: Dustin Poirier believes airport arrest could have been blessing in disguise
description: Former interim lightweight champion and fan favorite Dustin Poirier has struggled adjusting to life outside of fighting. "The Diamond" retired from competition following his UFC 318 loss to Max Hollway last July. His struggles were on full display on June 21. "The Diamond" was removed from a flight ...
fullText:
Former interim lightweight champion and fan favorite Dustin Poirier has struggled adjusting to life outside of fighting. "The Diamond" retired from competition following his UFC 318 loss to Max Hollway last July. His struggles were on full display on June 21.

"The Diamond" was removed from a flight for public intoxication at an Atlanta airport and arrested. Video of the incident went viral showing Poirier threatening police officers. A month after the arrest, Poirier say's he's 'good' and working on himself.

"Everything is good, man. I'm working on myself day in day out and it's a thing that's never fixed, you know? I've been dealing with mental stuff for years," Poirier said on The Ariel Helwani Show. "I'm doing everything I need to do. I have a great support system, and I'm good. Me and my family are good. Everything's good on my side."

While the incident was embarrassing and led to lost opportunities, Poirier believes maybe it needed to happen. It ended up being a wakeup call.

"Things happen. We live. We grow, and it's something that happened that's going to change my life. I know I've said this a few times on different shows, but it's something that maybe it needed to happen," Poirier said.

"Now my son, my daughter is never going to see me intoxicated like I saw my father. You know, it's just they're breaking a, breaking a family curse and I'm going to be the one to break it. It stops here."

"I don't believe it's something that's ever fixed. There's no eureka moment. There's no magic pill. There's no one day you wake up and the mental hurdles are gone. It's something you have to live with and work on every day. Every single day I have to read and write and do the things that I learned through therapy that made me feel better, that helped me control my thoughts. I just go away from it once I started feeling well mentally," he said.

title: Warriors Proposed to Land Trey Murphy in 3-Team Trade As LeBron Moves On
description: With LeBron James dream nearly dead, this Warriors trade pitch lands Trey Murphy III, the Bay Area franchise's coveted "dream" target. The post Warriors Proposed to Land Trey Murphy in 3-Team Trade As LeBron Moves On appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
Amid increasing chatter of LeBron James narrowing his free-agency options to the Miami Heat and Cleveland Cavaliers, the time has arrived for the Golden State Warriors to explore other avenues to improve their roster around Stephen Curry and Draymond Green.

Enter Trey Murphy III: a player who has hovered on the Warriors’ radar for years and remains a widely cited “dream target” who could now be up for grabs.

According to The Athletic’s John Hollinger, rival teams are expected to gauge the New Orleans Pelicans‘ willingness to trade the sharpshooting wing once the LeBron James free-agency sweepstakes conclude.

“Chatter about a Trey Murphy trade, while lessened, hasn’t vanished and is one of many potential pieces of league business that could resurface once LeBron James’ next destination becomes clear,” Hollinger reported on Monday.

Amid the reports, a Warriors trade idea doing the rounds would see the franchise part with Brandin Podziemski, Moses Moody and multiple first-round picks (the Pelicans are reportedly seeking up to three) in order to land Murphy.

Warriors would receive: Trey Murphy III

Pelicans would receive: Moses Moody (via NTMLE), Myles Turner, unrestricted 2027 first-round pick (via GSW), 2029 first-round pick (via GSW) and 2031 first-round pick (via GSW)

Bucks would receive: Brandin Podziemski

Some would argue that giving up three first-round picks, Podz and Moody just for Murphy is an astronomical asking price. Unfortunately for the Warriors, they’re in a desperate situation as Stephen Curry will turn 39 midway through the 2026-27 season, and Draymond Green and Jimmy Butler III aren’t getting any younger.

A player like Murray — who thrives as a movement shooter — will obviously be the ideal fit next to Curry, Butler, Green and Porzingis, forming a potent starting five.

It’s also now widely accepted that neither Podziemski nor Moody have established themselves as key pieces of Golden State’s future core, making them dispensable.

The Pelicans were forced to bring Zion Williamson off the bench last season due to their frontcourt logjam, which includes Herbert Jones, Derik Queen, Yves Messi and Saddiq Bey. At some point, Murphy will likely be better off playing elsewhere, especially if not being utilized to the best of his abilities in New Orleans.

Also, the Pels don’t hurt their future at all by grabbing three first-round picks from Golden State. The move would actually open up the possibility of the franchise trading other veterans like Williamson, Jordan Poole, Dejounte Murray and Jones, and giving the keys to their coveted young duo of Queen and Jeremiah Fears.

One of the few teams still flush with cap space, the Bucks have nothing to lose by onboarding Podziemski, who hits restricted free agency next year. If Podz doesn’t gel with their young core of Ryan Rollins, Brayden Burries, Nate Ament, Kel’el Ware, Kasparas Jakučionis and Jaime Jaquez Jr, they can simply let him walk next year.

The southpaw has definitely flashed enough potential in his three years in Golden State to get a look-in from the Bucks or any other young team.

Like HEAVY's content? Be sure to follow us.

title: Fitness emergency! Nearly a dozen Indian stars called to CoE ahead of Sri Lanka tests
description: Nearly a dozen Indian cricketers, including Mohammed Siraj, Ravindra Jadeja, and Hardik Pandya, undergo mandatory fitness tests at the CoE ahead of the Sri Lanka Test tour.
fullText:
Fitness emergency! Nearly a dozen Indian stars called to CoE ahead of Sri Lanka tests originally appeared on Cricket News. Add Cricket News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Nearly a dozen Indian cricketers set to assemble at CoE this week.

Fitness checks planned before Test tour of Sri Lanka next month.

BCCI wants no further breakdowns ahead of packed 2027 calendar.

There has been a reported uptick in activity at the BCCI's Centre of Excellence in Bengaluru, with the facility gearing up to welcome a large group of contracted and shortlisted players over the coming days.

According to The Times of India, a dozen Indian cricketers are expected to be present at the facility by the end of this week, each there for slightly different reasons.

Several names are already stationed at the centre, including Hardik Pandya, Nitish Kumar Reddy, Varun Chakaravarthy, Harshit Rana, and Akash Deep. They are set to be joined shortly by Washington Sundar and a handful of others as preparations intensify before a gruelling calendar.

The BCCI wants to complete a round of fitness assessments for players in contention for next month's Test tour of Sri Lanka.

Mohammed Siraj is due to arrive at the facility this week, with Ravindra Jadeja potentially following soon after. Meanwhile, several targeted players, including Sarfaraz Khan, Musheer Khan, and Abhishek Porel, have already completed their evaluations, and staff at the centre anticipate even more arrivals over the coming days, particularly before these cricketers head off to represent their respective zonal sides in the Duleep Trophy.

MORE: No update in fitness SOP since 2023: BCCI CoE under the scanner after fitness issues in India camp

The BCCI is determined not to leave anything to chance regarding player fitness ahead of the Sri Lanka Test series, with comprehensive assessments planned before selectors finalise their squad for the two-match series.

"Hardik has been there training, Reddy is recovering, and Varun and Harshit are undergoing their rehabilitation," a source told ToI.

"The team management doesn't want to take any chances with regard to its players ahead of the Sri Lanka Test series.
Thorough assessments will be carried out, and the selectors will be informed about their status before the team is picked for the two Tests. The CoE has a fair idea of the targeted players after the red-ball tournament in Bengaluru."

India continue to grapple with fitness concerns as they build toward the 2027 World Cup. Naturally, the last thing the setup wants is further injury breakdowns either before or during the crucial upcoming Test series.

Most centrally contracted players are staring down an extremely packed schedule ahead, spanning all three formats, including an extended tour of New Zealand comprising five T20Is, five ODIs, and two Tests.

Taking this into consideration, the management is keen on maintaining a wide, fully assessed pool of players ready to be called upon, since multiple squads will likely need to be fielded simultaneously across different assignments in the months ahead.

MORE: Tilak Varma reveals how playing mostly in Asia exposed India's batting struggles in T20I series

This proactive round of fitness testing feels like a long-overdue correction rather than routine practice, especially after a year littered with hamstring strains, quad injuries, and rushed comebacks.

Gathering nearly a dozen players together for thorough assessment before a Test series, rather than scrambling after an injury strikes mid-tour, signals the board finally treating fitness as a planning priority instead of an afterthought. What will matter most going forward, though, is whether this becomes a consistent practice tied to every major assignment, rather than a one-off response triggered by recent embarrassment in England.

With a brutal, multi-format calendar stretching well into the World Cup year, India simply cannot afford another wave of last-minute withdrawals. This week's activity at the CoE looks like a genuine step toward accountability, but only sustained follow-through will prove whether real lessons have actually been learned.

For all the latest cricket news, opinion, and commentary and to share your voice, head to our Facebook, Instagram, and X (Twitter) pages.

title: Posts falsely claim Lamine Yamal snubbed Donald Trump after World Cup win
description: Spain star Lamine Yamal did not avoid shaking Donald Trump's hand after the World Cup final, contrary to posts claiming the teenager snubbed the US president and speculating he may have done so because of US policy regarding Palestinians. The posts cited as proof a video of the interaction filmed from behind Yamal that partially obscures Trump, but AFP photos and footage of the post-match ceremony show the pair did shake hands.
fullText:
Spain star Lamine Yamal did not avoid shaking Donald Trump's hand after the World Cup final, contrary to posts claiming the teenager snubbed the US president and speculating he may have done so because of US policy regarding Palestinians. The posts cited as proof a video of the interaction filmed from behind Yamal that partially obscures Trump, but AFP photos and footage of the post-match ceremony show the pair did shake hands.

"Lamine Yamal did not shake hands with Trump; he avoided the US president," says the Bengali-language caption of a YouTube video shared on July 20, 2026.

The video, which has been viewed more than 334,000 times, appears to show Spain's 19-year-old winger Lamine Yamal going to collect his World Cup winner's medal. He is first met by FIFA head Gianni Infantino before briefly stopping in front of US President Donald Trump, who drapes the medal over Yamal's head.

According to the video's Bengali-language voiceover, Yamal did not shake Trump's hand. It speculates this may be because of Trump's "foreign policy towards Palestinians".

The clip was also shared in similar YouTube and Facebook posts after Spain's 1-0 win over Argentina in the World Cup final on July 19, with the US president then presenting the World Cup trophy to La Roja ( archived link ).

"Lamine Yamal did the right thing 100 percent," says a comment on one of the posts, while another says "Thanks Spain, and thanks Yamal."

There is strong support for the Palestinian people in Muslim-majority Bangladesh, and the government reaffirmed its "steadfast commitment to the legitimate rights of the Palestinian people and the establishment of an independent and sovereign State of Palestine" in July ( archived link ).

Yamal has also expressed support for the Palestinian cause in the past, most recently during his club side Barcelona's title celebrations in May when he waved a Palestinian flag during an open-top bus parade ( archived link ).

Trump, meanwhile, has firmly backed Israel despite growing international outrage over its offensive in the Palestinian territory of Gaza, in response to the October 7, 2023 attacks carried out by Gaza-based militants Hamas ( archived link ).

There was an international  backlash when Trump said he wanted to take control of the Gaza strip and "permanently" resettle Palestinians in other countries ( archived link ).

The US president's Gaza peace plan -- which was endorsed by the UN Security Council in November 2025 -- does, however, include a path to a future Palestinian state ( archived link ).

The claims circulating on social media that Yamal avoided shaking Trump's hand are false, with the angle the falsely shared video appearing to have obscured the handshake.

Several  AFP photos , taken from a different angle to the falsely shared video, show  Yamal and Trump shaking hands as the teenager received his winner's medal.

Moreover, a  keyword search on Google led to similar footage shared on the verified YouTube channels of the White House and Fox Sports showing the pair shaking hands (archived  here and here ).

title: 'Rogers departs on a high... but reinvestment critical for next chapter'
description: Aston Villa are no strangers to selling some of their most prized assets.  It wasn't too long ago that the club parted ways with academy darling Jack Grealish for a then British record £100m.  More recently, there were PSR-juggling deals for Douglas Luiz and Jacob Ramsey.
fullText:
Aston Villa are no strangers to selling some of their most prized assets.

It wasn't too long ago that the club parted ways with academy darling Jack Grealish for a then British record £100m. More recently, there were PSR-juggling deals for Douglas Luiz and Jacob Ramsey.

While Morgan Rogers' departure is another bitter reminder of the financial gap between Villa and their peers, it is a mammoth profit on a young player at the opportune moment.

Rogers arrived at Villa as a relative unknown - a Manchester City academy graduate with a year in the Championship under his belt. He was thrown in at the deep end and quickly became a guaranteed starter under Unai Emery.

In his two and a half years in claret and blue, Rogers amassed well in excess of 100 appearances, more than 50 goal contributions, a PFA Young Player of the Year award and, most importantly, a European trophy.

There is no doubt Emery is losing one of the most exciting young British talents of the past few years. The Midlands boy has had a significant impact on the team, and for that he will be etched into club history.

However, for too long, those in charge at Villa have failed to capitalise on player values at the right time. The £117m fee, with a portion heading back to Middlesborough, is a sum that helps towards a partial squad rebuild, with Johan Manzambi and Joao Gomes already through the doors.

This comes at a pivotal moment, too, as Emery looks to build on last season's European glory. The squad that finished the last campaign was aging, with many players on high wages and running deep into their contracts. A refresh of faces felt inevitable.

Football is a business, now more than ever, and there is very little room for sentimentality.

For fans, Rogers departs on a high, and leaves behind a plethora of unforgettable memories. From here, recruitment and reinvestment of receipts will be critical for the next chapter of success at Aston Villa.

Find more from Hannah Gowen at UTV

title: The carpenter aiming for Commonwealth boxing glory
description: BBC Sport meets Tommy Teers, who is balancing his job as a carpenter with training to box at the 2026 Commonwealth Games for Guernsey.
fullText:
But by night the 28-year-old trains in the gym with the aim of bringing boxing glory to Guernsey at the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow.

"I started boxing just to keep fit and to get to this stage is crazy," Teers tells BBC Guernsey as he take a break from training in the gym.

"I've put the hard work in and everyone around me has supported me, so I feel like it's time."

But the hard work for Teers is not just confined to the gym, it is what he does during the day as he balances his sport and carpentry.

Guernsey select team for 2026 Commonwealth Games

He boxes three nights a week, does strength and conditioning every night in-between before either doing sprints or a long run at the weekend.

"It's hard balancing everything, but I'm doing what I can," he says.

"The guys I'll be fighting, they're full-time athletes, but I can't afford to do that.

"I've got my business to run and that's kind of what I want to do, so doing this will be a good experience for me to see what level I can actually get to."

Teers and Niall Adams will fight for Guernsey as part of their 20-strong team going to Glasgow.

The bantamweight has twice reached the English Elite National semi-finals and has fought Ellis Trowbridge, who has been selected to represent England in Glasgow.

"I'm just going to give it my all, perform as best as I can that's all I can ask for really," he says.

"I know the competition's going to be hard, it's definitely a step up for me.

"But that's what I want, I want to see how good I can get and how I can compete against those guys."

title: 'Chelsea are the biggest club in London' - Rogers
description: New Chelsea signing Morgan Rogers says he is "really excited" about "where the club is heading" under new manager Xabi Alonso.  The Blues completed the signing of England midfielder Rogers from Aston Villa for a British record transfer fee of £117m on Monday.  It had been widely expected that Rogers, who was Villa's most prized asset, would be available only for an English transfer record fee, but many expected Arsenal to be the frontrunners.
fullText:
New Chelsea signing Morgan Rogers says he is "really excited" about "where the club is heading" under new manager Xabi Alonso.

The Blues completed the signing of England midfielder Rogers from Aston Villa for a British record transfer fee of £117m on Monday.

It had been widely expected that Rogers, who was Villa's most prized asset, would be available only for an English transfer record fee, but many expected Arsenal to be the frontrunners.

However, Chelsea had been tracking Rogers for more than two years and had quietly made him their top attacking target with a combined effort involving the club's sporting directors, Alonso and co-owner Behdad Eghbali helping to secure a deal.

"I'm so excited," Rogers said on the move.

"For me, Chelsea are the biggest club in London and a club I've always admired since I was a kid.

"I'm really excited about the project with the new manager, the players we've got and where the club is heading. That's why I'm here and I can't wait to get started.

"The way the club wants to go and how it wants to move forward, it's something I want to be a part of and I think it was just perfect for me.

"I'm a free spirit, free-flowing footballer. I like to take risks, make things happen, change games. Whatever that may take. Just can't wait to get started."

title: 'Thank you for all the unforgettable memories' - Anderson says goodbye
description: Elliot Anderson has posted an emotional goodbye to Nottingham Forest as he prepares to join Manchester City.  Earlier in July, City confirmed an agreement has been reached to sign Anderson, stating the "formalities of the move will be finalised upon his return to England" after the World Cup.  BBC Sport reported a deal had been put in place on 25 June.
fullText:
Elliot Anderson has posted an emotional goodbye to Nottingham Forest as he prepares to join Manchester City.

Earlier in July, City confirmed an agreement has been reached to sign Anderson, stating the "formalities of the move will be finalised upon his return to England" after the World Cup. BBC Sport reported a deal had been put in place on 25 June.

The 23-year-old made 92 appearances across two seasons at Forest after joining from boyhood club Newcastle in 2024, helping them qualify for Europe in 2024-25 and reach the Europa League semi-finals the following year.

The goodbye message posted by Anderson on social media read: "Forest family,

"Firstly I am so grateful for the love and support you have shown me over the last 2 years especially towards the end of this season. From the minute I walked through the door I fell in love with this club.

"I want to thank Mr Marinakas, the staff and all players for welcoming me and making my time here so enjoyable, this club gave me the opportunity to grow as a player and a person.

"Bringing the club European football again after 29 years is what all of you fans deserved and to be a helping hand in that is so special.

"Nottingham forest will always have a special place in my heart. Thank you for all the unforgettable memories."

title: The most expensive football transfers of all time
description: Chelsea have finalised a deal to sign Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa for a fee of £117m. The transfer will be a guaranteed sum and make Rogers the most expensive English footballer of all time, along...
fullText:
Chelsea have finalised a deal to sign Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa for a fee of £117m. The transfer will be a guaranteed sum and make Rogers the most expensive English footballer of all time, alongside one of the most expensive in football history.

The deal followsElliot Anderson's £116m transfer from Nottingham Forest to Manchester City earlier this summer, as transfer fees continue to reach new heights.

Both deals still fall somewhat short of the world record.Paris Saint-Germain obliterated the world transfer record to sign Neymar from Barcelona in 2017 for a staggering £200m. Almost a decade on, the Brazilian's big-money move remains the benchmark.

All figures are guaranteed transfer fees exclusive of potential add-ons

1. Neymar - Barcelona to Paris Saint-Germain, 2017 (£200m)

2. Kylian Mbappe - AS Monaco to Paris Saint-Germain, 2018 (£162m)

3. Alexander Isak - Newcastle United to Liverpool, 2025 (£125m)

4. Morgan Rogers - Aston Villa to Chelsea, 2026 (£117m)

5. Elliot Anderson - Nottingham Forest to Manchester City, 2026 (£116m)

6. Joao Felix - Benfica to Atletico Madrid, 2019 (£113m)

7. Antoine Griezmann - Atletico Madrid to Barcelona, 2019 (£107m)

8. Enzo Fernandez - Benfica to Chelsea, 2023 (£106.7m)

9. Philippe Coutinho - Liverpool to Barcelona, 2018 (£105m)

10. Jack Grealish - Aston Villa to Manchester City, 2021 (£100m)

11. Moises Caicedo - Brighton to Chelsea, 2023 (£100m)

title: “Chelsea are the biggest club in London”: Morgan Rogers seals £117m move and vows “I can’t wait to get started”
description: Morgan Rogers has said Chelsea are the biggest club in London after signing for the Blues in a £117m club record deal.Rogers has completed his transfer to Chelsea from Aston Villa in a deal that make...
fullText:
Morgan Rogers has said Chelsea are the biggest club in London after signing for the Blues in a £117m club record deal.

Rogers has completed his transfer to Chelsea from Aston Villa in a deal that makes the 23-year-old the most expensive English footballer of all time.

The England international arrives at Stamford Bridge after helping England to third place at the 2026 World Cup this summer.

He has established himself as one of the most promising talents in thePremier League in recent seasons, earning the PFA Young Player of the Year in 2024/25.

Last season, he was named the Europa League Player of the Season after helping Aston Villa end a 30-year trophy drought with success in Europe.

He scored 10 goals and created six 

"I'm so excited," Rogers toldchelseafc.com.

"For me, Chelsea are the biggest club in London and a club I've always admired since I was a kid.

"I'm really excited about the project with the new manager, the players we've got and where the club is heading. That's why I'm here and I can't wait to get started."

title: Real Madrid defender’s physical trainer assures he wants to stay despite exit rumours: ‘He wants to play a key role and succeed’
description: Raul Asencio has no intention of walking away from Real Madrid despite growing uncertainty surrounding his future at the Santiago Bernabeu. The young defender continues to be linked with a summer exi...
fullText:
Raul Asencio has no intention of walking away from Real Madrid despite growing uncertainty surrounding his future at the Santiago Bernabeu.

The young defender continues to be linked with a summer exit after reportedly being informed by Jose Mourinho that he is not part of the manager’s plans for the upcoming season.

While speculation over his future has intensified in recent weeks, those closest to the 23-year-old insist his mindset has never changed.

Rather than looking for a move elsewhere, Asencio remains focused on earning his place at Real Madrid and proving he deserves an opportunity under Mourinho.

Speaking in an interview with AS, the defender’s physical trainer, Luiso Hernandez, has now shed light on Asencio’s mentality ahead of a crucial pre-season.

Asencio has been at the centre of transfer speculation throughout the summer following reports that Mourinho does not see him as part of his plans for next season.

Asencio is linked with a summer exit from Real Madrid. (Photo by Jose Manuel Alvarez Rey/Getty Images)

Despite that setback, Hernandez revealed the defender has continued preparing with complete focus, refusing to allow rumours surrounding his future to affect his mentality.

“The key has been to treat the situation as normal.

“To understand that Real Madrid is the most demanding environment in the world and that, when you’re at Madrid, you’re exposed to the possibility of this sort of thing happening.”

The trainer explained that uncertainty is part of life at one of the biggest clubs in world football, making mental resilience just as important as physical preparation.

“The reality is that, despite all the external noise that might be generated, you’re here today and you might not be here tomorrow,” he began saying.

Hernandez also made it clear that Asencio has never considered giving up on his dream of making it at Real Madrid.

The defender wishes to succeed at Santiago Bernabeu. (Photo by Angel Martinez/Getty Images)

“He has always made it clear to me that he wants to stay at Madrid, play a key role and succeed.

“That’s why we’ve worked to ensure he’s in the best possible shape for this pre-season, which is a new and different situation for all the players at Madrid.”

The trainer believes the arrival of a new coaching staff offers every player an opportunity to impress, regardless of what happened during the previous campaign.

“Although last season wasn’t the best, the picture is now completely different because everyone is starting on an equal footing.

“Beyond that, I believe Raul’s profile fits perfectly with the new coaching staff’s vision,” he concluded.

title: Man in court accused of racist post about Lioness
description: The 19-year-old is accused of using racist language in a post about the footballer.
fullText:
A teenager has appeared in court charged with making a racist comment on Instagram about England defender Jess Carter.

Aaryan Agarwal allegedly sent the footballer a message in July last year, during Women's Euro 2025.

The 19-year-old of Dickens Rise, Chigwell, is charged with a single count of sending a letter, communication, or article conveying an indecent or offensive message.

He appeared at Colchester Magistrates' Court on Tuesday but did not enter a plea. He is due to attend Chelmsford Crown Court on 18 August.

Magistrates were told the message was sent on 17 July, when England won against Sweden in their quarter-final.

The post allegedly referred to Carter using racist language.

Agarwal was arrested in September after the message was reported.

Carter, who is from Warwick, was part of the England squad who created history by winning their first major women's tournament at Euro 2022.

The 28-year-old spent six years at Chelsea before moving to US-based side Gotham FC in 2024.

Correction 22 July: This article was corrected to clarify that the charge relates to one message, rather than the previously reported two.

title: Real Madrid schedule first preseason match under Jose Mourinho
description: Jose Mourinho has been training the available Real Madrid players for some days now, and even the first player who participated in the World Cup, Arda Guler, has also joined up with him at Valdebebas....
fullText:
Jose Mourinho has been training the available Real Madrid players for some days now, and even the first player who participated in the World Cup, Arda Guler, has also joined up with him at Valdebebas. The new manager has also called up several youth team players to train under him as well.

After Guler’s return, several other players are expected to join the Portuguese trainer in the coming few days. Federico Valverde, whose Uruguay side was also eliminated in the group stage, like Guler’s Turkiye, will be coming back soon, as will be Antonio Rudiger, whose German side was eliminated in the Round of 32.

However, it remains to be seen whether they will be ready and available for the first training match of the preseason, which is scheduled in only a few days’ time. As MARCA has reported, Jose Mourinho will play his first match with Real Madrid against Alcorcon, this Friday, 24 July.

This is going to be a training match, and not a friendly, and thus, will be played behind closed doors. Unlimited substitutions will be allowed, as this will be the first training match after the holidays for players, and the managers will also want to test several of their players and setups.

Alcorcon are also looking to debut in their new season kits, which are expected to be unveiled on Thursday, in this training match against Real Madrid. As is the case with Jose Mourinho, the Alcorcon manager, Fran Justo, will also be overseeing this game as the first of his stint on his team’s bench.

Although it is not going to be a competitive game, it will provide a good opportunity for several Real Madrid youngsters to showcase their talents to Jose Mourinho, and thus make a place for themselves in the coach’s plans for the upcoming season.

title: Heptathlete Woodward ready to make Jersey history
description: BBC Sport meets Lucy Woodward, the first-ever heptathlete to represent Jersey at the Commonwealth Games.
fullText:
"I'm just a Jack of all trades," jokes Lucy Woodward as she trains at Jersey's athletics track for the final time before the 2026 Commonwealth Games.

The 23-year-old is set to make history in Glasgow as the first woman from Jersey to compete in a multi-event discipline in the heptathlon later this week.

She is part of a five-strong athletics team from Jersey which sees decathlete Evan Campbell joined by sprinters Zach Saunders, Steve MacKay and Jamie Oldham.

"I don't have any standout events, I always thought if I was good enough at something it might be an easier life to specialise," she tells BBC Jersey at the FB Fields athletics track where she first started multi-eventing aged 10.

"I just think the training is so diverse, every day is different. I like that you can have one bad event and then you can make up for it and it's the most interesting event if you ask me."

Glasgow 2026 'a dream come true' for Jersey's Woodward

Nowacki heads Jersey's 2026 Commonwealth Games team

In an event where being a full-time athlete is a huge advantage, Woodward has to balance her training with a full-time job as an architect in London.

It means training six days a week on top of a full-time job and using any spare holidays for competitions, but it is a life she relishes.

"I've grown up balancing this my whole life through A-levels, GCSEs, university and it just kind of becomes a part of like who you are, part of my routine," she added.

"My coaches are very accommodating and they often do very late days at the track waiting for me to finish work.

"I think the thing with me is I really love my job and I really love my training, so if you love both things just as much then the long days and lack of downtime are worth it."

Qualifying for a heptathlon at a major games is not easy.

Unlike individual events there are not many heptathlons at the required standard that you can compete in, making opportunities to reach the qualifying mark few and far between.

Woodward had to travel to Germany in May to find a suitable event where she scored a personal best of 5391 - just short of the 5400 she needed.

"Going into that weekend I was going 'I have to pretty much ace every event to get this standard and that's the only opportunity I have'," she said.

"The whole way through the competition I was like 'I did this event, now I'm recalculating my score to figure out what I need to do', and then ultimately it came down to I had to run a really fast 800m because I messed up my javelin.

"I still did a huge PB, but after javelin I was faced with the situation of to get 5,300 points I have to run quicker than 2:17 and to get 5,400 I have to run 2:10 and my PB was 2.13.

"So that is a pretty stressful environment to be in and I was running it on my own as none of the girls had a PB lower than 2:18.

"But I crossed the line in 2:11 and that is probably the most proud I've ever been of the way that I ran that race.

"I hadn't got the standard, but I was pretty sure I'd proved myself enough, so it was sit and wait and hope that it was enough."

It proved to be enough and a few weeks later she got confirmation that she would be selected for the Jersey team.

It opens up the type of opportunity that rarely comes for athletes from the island - and a first-ever major championships for Woodward.

"This is going to be huge for me, I've never stepped into the stadium full of this many people," she added.

"But ultimately me and my coach were just saying the hard work's done, the most anxiety-inducing thing was probably my qualifying heptathlon where I had one opportunity to get this score and then otherwise I'd have to wait another four years."

So what are Woodward's goals as she prepares to compete against heptathletes including double world champion Katarina Johnson-Thompson, World Championship silver medallist Kate O'Connor and 2022 Commonwealth Games bronze medallist Jade O'Dowda?

"I think I have more long-term targets - ultimately I want to score 6,000, that's a big mark in heptathlon," Woodward added.

"I want to go to the next Commonwealth Games and be more competitive and hopefully get to Gotzis which is a massive heptathlon competition.

"So I'm using Glasgow as kind of like a stepping stone, I just want to go and have the time of my life - which I think I will - hopefully get some good PBs, use the environment, use the girls, they're going to be fast to hopefully carry me to PBs.

"If I can come away with a handful of PBs and an overall heptathlon PB I'll have done my job, and I will be very happy."

title: Richard Hughes now just waiting for green light to sign 'incredible' star for Liverpool
description: Liverpool have only made one signing so far this summer. Indeed, Jeremy Jacquet will also be featuring for Liverpool for the first time ever this season but, since his deal was agreed in January, it d...
fullText:
Liverpool have only made one signing so far this summer.

Indeed, Jeremy Jacquet will also be featuring for Liverpool for the first time ever this season but, since his deal was agreed in January, it doesn't really feel like the Reds can count it as a new transfer.

Giovanni Leoni is also going to feel like a new signing but, since neither Leoni nor Jacquet have ever played in the Premier League, it's unclear how well they'll do.

And, since the Reds' lack of depth came back to bite them last term, it's clear that, if Liverpool want to start challenging for silverware again, they still need to sign at least four or five more players.

This is probably why so many players have been linked with an Anfield switch already this summer and, since so many of their Premier League rivals want to sign the same players that the Reds do, there's a lot of competition for every player that they try and go for.

So, the fact that the Reds are now just waiting for the green light to make their move for one of their long-term targets is hardly surprising.

As mentioned, even though Liverpool have four centre-back options among their ranks, the fact that two of them haven't played a single minute in the Premier League is rather worrying.

And, even though Joe Gomez was one of the Premier League's best centre-backs at one point, the fact that he's been so injury-prone over the last couple of years makes him feel rather unreliable.

So, with this in mind, it's hardly surprising to see the Anfield outfit get linked with a new centre-back almost every day.

That is, especially since Virgil van Dijk is now 35 years old and only has a year left on his current deal.

Many players have been linked with Liverpool but now, a fresh update from Fichajes has emerged which may outline who Hughes' main defensive target is this summer.

"Liverpool's transfer activity is heating up, as the English club has added Ilia Zabarnyi to its list of options to reinforce the center of defense this summer," they wrote.

"The Ukrainian international belongs to Paris Saint-Germain , but his name is gaining traction at Anfield for an important reason: Richard Hughes and Andoni Iraola are familiar with his performance and adaptation to the Premier League after working with him at Bournemouth.

"There are no advanced negotiations underway, although everything could change if the French club agrees to consider his departure."

Open an account and place a qualifying bet of £10 at min odds of 1/5 (1.2) or greater.

18+New UK/ROI customers. Min £5 deposit. Bet Credits available on settlement of qualifying bets, capped at £10 stake. Min odds 1/5 (1.20). Bet Credits expire in 7 days. Excl. Cashed Out/Free Bets/Boosted odds. T&Cs apply. GambleAware.org

Whether Liverpool like it or not, they need a new centre-back.

As mentioned, Van Dijk is entering the final year of his contract and, the other three options that Iraola has aren't filling fans with much confidence.

Indeed, Jacquet and Leoni are two of the biggest centre-back prospects in the world and, each of them could become just as good as Van Dijk if they reach their potential.

But, at the moment, since both of them are under the age of 21, it's unclear whether they'll be ready to make the step up to the Premier League this season or not.

And, if the Reds are wanting to better their end of season result in the 2026-27 campaign, they need to give themselves the best chance possible to do this.

Iraola's appointment will obviously give the club a boost but, if FSG don't back him, they're going to run into the same problems again as, knowing Liverpool, their players are bound to suffer multiple injuries this term.

Zabarnyi has already played under Iraola so, the Ukranian joining the club would instantly know how the Spaniard wants him to play.

The centre-back would also be able to help other members of Liverpool's squad to understand Iraola's playing style and, since he already has a decent chunk of Premier League experience, he knows what it takes to compete in the world's toughest league.

Here's hoping Liverpool get themselves into gear and get a deal for the 'incredible' defender over the line this summer.

title: Malen and Ndicka join Roma’s preseason
description: Everything is ready for the start of Roma’s new season, which will celebrate its centenary and its return to the Champions League after a seven-year absence. A summer of hard work awaits Gian Piero ...
fullText:
Everything is ready for the start of Roma’s new season, which will celebrate its centenary and its return to the Champions League after a seven-year absence.

A summer of hard work awaits Gian Piero Gasperini’s team, who will now have two key players at their disposal.

Malen and Evan Ndicka will begin their training camp with the Giallorossi club today. Both are returning from the World Cup, which the Ivorian experienced on the bench due to the injury sustained in the derby at the end of May.

Despite this, he was called up but did not appear in any of the four matches played by the Ivory Coast. Malen, on the other hand, returns from a World Cup in which he never really featured.

His statistics are not the best: zero goals in four games and his relationship with coach Koeman has never really taken off, given also the latter’s choice of the Roma striker, forced to play as a winger from the second match onwards instead of a centre forward, with Koeman preferring Brobbey.

The two players, after undergoing the usual medicals, should be available as early as tomorrow, when the Roman club will immediately take to the field for the test against Trastevere.

title: Positive signs suggest Schalke will secure Lindstrøm deal
description: According to information from WAZ newspaper, FC Schalke 04 are making rapid progress in their pursuit of Jesper Lindstrøm.There has been "clear progress" in recent days in negotiations with both the ...
fullText:
According to information from WAZ newspaper, FC Schalke 04 are making rapid progress in their pursuit of Jesper Lindstrøm.

There has been "clear progress" in recent days in negotiations with both the versatile attacking player and his employers SSC Napoli.

The 18-time Danish international is under contract in Italy until 2028, but is expected to leave the club this summer.

The regional newspaper suggests a loan with an option or even an obligation to buy if the team avoids relegation is possible.

Last season, the 26-year-old played on loan for VfL Wolfsburg but struggled to make an impact with no attacking returns.

WAZ believe it's unlikely that Lindstrøm will join the newly promoted Bundesliga team at their training camp, which they left for on Sunday.

However, the chances are generally good that Schalke will pull off another transfer coup and one with German topflight experience.

title: Dodgers Snap Skid With ‘Playoff-Style’ Win Capped by Heads-Up Double Play
description: The Los Angeles Dodgers battled the elements and made one great play to end both their game in Philadelphia and their brief two-game losing skid. The Dodgers executed an impressive 5-2-6-4 double play that ended their 2-1 victory over the Philadelphia Phillies at a rain-soaked Citizens Bank Park on Tuesday. The Dodgers became the first […] The post Dodgers Snap Skid With ‘Playoff-Style’ Win Capped by Heads-Up Double Play appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
The Los Angeles Dodgers battled the elements and made one great play to end both their game in Philadelphia and their brief two-game losing skid.

The Dodgers executed an impressive 5-2-6-4 double play that ended their 2-1 victory over the Philadelphia Phillies at a rain-soaked Citizens Bank Park on Tuesday.

The Dodgers became the first team to reach 64 wins this season and evened the three-game series after falling 10-7 to the Phillies on Monday.

Justin Wrobleski picked up his 11th victory, and Tanner Scott got five outs, and cheated death by inducing the game-ending double play, to pick up his 15th save of the season.

It all turned out well for the Dodgers but not before Scott pitched them into a huge jam in the ninth inning.

After retiring Kyle Schwarber and Bryce Harper to end the eighth inning, Scott gave up a single to Alec Bohm then a double to Phillies catcher J.T. Realmuto, setting the Phillies up with runners on second and third with only one out.

But Scott induced Brandon Marsh to hit a bouncer to Max Muncy, who ran at pinch runner Justin Crawford, trapping him in a rundown before throwing the ball to catcher Dalton Rushing.

https://x.com/SportsNetLA/status/2079755637383377286?s=20

“It’s just fundamentals,” Dodgers manager Dave Roberts said. “Max did a great job securing the baseball and understood that the tying run was going home and secured it and ran [Crawford] hard back.”

Realmuto had reached third base but retreated back to second after Crawford went back to third. So after shortstop Mookie Betts tagged Crawford, he threw a strike to second baseman Tommy Edman, who placed a perfect tag on Realmuto, ending the game

“I knew Crawford was fast,” Scott told Spectrum Sportsnet LA. “Muncy got the ball to Rushing, and Rushing had to get it to Mookie, and next thing I know I see JT going back, and Mookie made a hell of a play.”

“That play in itself was a huge play, ended the game,” Roberts said. “Fortunately, [Realmuto] made an uncharacteristic baserunning mistake, and fortunately Mookie threw a dart to second base.”

Muncy, who also homered in the game and tied Ron Cey for second on the Dodgers’ all-time list, was more satisfied with starting the double play than making history.

“The defensive play means more to me than the homer,” Muncy said. “For me, the work I’ve been putting in on defense, anytime I can make an impact on defense, those are way more important to me.”

The Monday night game between the NL powers was sloppily played. It had 17 runs, and the Dodgers left nine on base. Phillies shortstop Trea Turner should have had three errors himself, even though he only ended the game with only one.

But even though Tuesday’s game was delayed by 1 hour and 20 minutes, it had big October vibes. Wrobleski, who pitched in Philadelphia in the All-Star Game last week, squared off against Zack Wheeler, who was named an all-star but did not participate, and each starter delivered.

Even Roberts could sense the playoff vibes which, fortunately for the Dodgers, resulted in similar fashion to last year, when LA eked out a four-game series win in the NLDS.

“That was a playoff environment,” Roberts said. “I think you can tell how badly we wanted it, how bad they wanted it. You’ve got two heavyweight teams going toe to toe, and we didn’t give anything away. I was really proud of the way we competed tonight.”

Like HEAVY's content? Be sure to follow us.

This article was originally published on HEAVY

The post Dodgers Snap Skid With ‘Playoff-Style’ Win Capped by Heads-Up Double Play appeared first on HEAVY.

title: Garnacho grows impatient with Roma, nears Aston Villa
description: Alejandro Garnacho will not wear the Roma jersey. After waiting for the Giallorossi, the Argentine is now one step away from joining Aston Villa. Another target for Roma’s attack has slipped away. A...
fullText:
Alejandro Garnacho will not wear the Roma jersey.

After waiting for the Giallorossi, the Argentine is now one step away from joining Aston Villa.

Another target for Roma’s attack has slipped away. Alejandro Garnacho, in fact, as reported by Fabrizio Romano on his YouTube channel, has finally chosen Aston Villa.

The Chelsea Argentine had waited two weeks for the Giallorossi, only to then decide to accept the offer from Emery’s club.

Now it’s a total crisis for D’Amico and his staff, who must give at least two strikers to Gian Piero Gasperini.

title: Five big names to look out for at Glasgow 2026
description: With 215 gold medals up for grabs, across 10 sports at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games, BBC Scotland takes a look at some of the biggest names searching for a slab of metal.
fullText:
With 215 golds up for grabs at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games, highlighting just five medal hopefuls is no easy feat.

Scotland's largest city is set to welcome more than 3,000 athletes from 10 sports, all of whom have the ambition of leaving with a slab of metal resting on their chest.

For some, a podium-finish is paramount. The bare minimum. For others, competing at the Games is a win in itself.

Much has been made of the reduced programme, the city's readiness and the 'what next?' once Glasgow has passed on the hosting baton again, but for now, the attention - finally - is fully on the athletes.

Here, BBC Scotland takes a look at some of the biggest names battling it out in the upcoming Commonwealth Games.

Is Glasgow ready for the Commonwealth Games - and does the city care?

Can Wales find new Commonwealth legends in Glasgow?

Keep up to date with the Commonwealth Games here

We couldn't start with anyone else, could we?

Even before he walked the walk after talking the talk, new mile world-record holder Josh Kerr was pictured as the poster boy for his home Games.

The brazen - arguably gallus - boy from Edinburgh has won the hearts of many Scots, and athletics fans generally, in recent years for his no-nonsense, bullish approach on and off the track.

He set out his stall months ago, targeting the men's mile world record - which had stood for 27 years - at London's Diamond League on Saturday.

Kerr didn't just break it, he smashed it by nearly half a second, running a simply sublime three minutes 42.66 seconds.

The Scot now heads to his homeland for his second Games appearance, competing in the Commonwealth Mile in search of his first medal for Team Scotland.

The 28-year-old told BBC Scotland earlier this year that "gold in Scotland is what I grew up thinking about" and you'd be a brave soul to wager against him when he's in this mood.

Men's one mile final: Scotstoun Stadium on Saturday, 1 August.

It's hard to believe Kadeena Cox has not yet competed at a Commonwealth Games.

The para-cyclist, who won gold in athletics and cycling at the 2016 Paralympics in Rio, has missed the last two Commonwealth Games because of injury.

But the 35-year-old, who was a contestant on 'I'm A Celebrity... Get Me Out of Here' in 2021 after winning Celebrity Masterchef earlier that year, will be hopeful of cooking up a storm at the ever-rowdy Sir Chris Hoy Velodrome for Team England.

In an expanded para track cycling programme, Cox will compete in the C4-5 event with her sights set on another podium finish.

Cox made the watching world sit up and take notice 10 years ago in Brazil. Sure to be well-backed in Glasgow, it'd be well on brand for the vivacious Cox to star in the Glasgow velodrome.

Women's C4-5 4000m individual pursuit final: Sir Chris Hoy Velodrome on Thursday, 30 July.

When it comes to the Commonwealth Games, names don't get much bigger than Chad le Clos.

The South Africa swimmer is one medal away from becoming the most decorated male athlete in the Games' history. His current haul of 18 - seven gold, four silver and a further seven bronze - is not bad going.

And a whack of them were collected in Glasgow 12 years ago when he equalled the legendary Ian Thorpe's record of seven medals in one Games.

At 34, Le Clos won't compete in as many races this time but is set to be on the blocks for the men's 50m and 100m butterfly events.

He won gold in the 100m butterfly here back in 2014, while he pocketed a bronze in the 50m, but he got a gold in that on the Gold Coast in 2018.

Any medal will do for the man from Durban, and where else than Glasgow, following his remarkable show years ago, than to make history once more.

Men's 50m butterfly final: Tollcross Swimming Centre on Wednesday, 29 July.

Men's 100m butterfly final: Tollcross Swimming Centre on Sunday, 26 July.

This rising superstar is more a 'if you don't know, get to know' athlete.

Remember the name: Seine Stowers.

A junior Commonwealth Games champion, Stowers marked her 18th birthday earlier this year with four gold medals - breaking 12 records along the way - at the Universal Weightlifting Championship in her homeland of Samoa.

Competing in the women's 77kg - as she will in Glasgow - she lifted 142kg in the clean and jerk category as she established herself as one of Samoa's most exciting medal prospects for years.

Weightlifting runs in the family, too, with her sister, Feagaiga, a gold medal winner in the Gold Coast and a silver medallist in Birmingham four years ago.

Already, though, Seine appears to be taking centre stage.

American Olivia Reeves holds the world record at 77kg in both snatch (123kg) and clean and jerk (155kg), but with Stowers on the scene, who knows for how much longer?

Women's weightlifting (77kg) final: SEC Armadillo on Wednesday, 29 July.

Rhys McClenaghan has done it all.

A European, World, Commonwealth and Olympic champion on the pommel horse, he is undeniably one of the sport's greats, but we've been warned to expect 'Rhys 2.0' in Glasgow.

Northern Ireland's gymnastics golden boy - who secured Ireland's first Olympic gymnastics medal with his gold in Paris two years ago - was forced into a spell on the sidelines last year with a shoulder injury, but he is ready to bring a new routine over to Glasgow.

"The Commonwealth Games is a good chance to show your work in progress. At the Olympic Games, you want to see your finished product," he said.

"It's going to be a work in progress, but hopefully one that is the best in the world too.

"Part of me is striving be like a younger Rhys, but the reality is I'm doing a completely new routine and the rule changes have pushed me into that direction, that's the Rhys 2.0 there."

McClenaghan won gold eight years ago, pipping England's Max Whitlock - who has been forced to withdraw from these Games with injury - before collecting silver in Birmingham four years ago.

He was the first gymnast to complete the Grand Slam and so arrives at these Games as one of Team NI's wise auld heids - despite only being 26 years old.

It's a frightening prospect for the rest of the field. Even more frightening is the fact he's rebooting for version 2.0.

Men's pommel horse final: The Arena on Monday, 27 July.

title: Barcelona considering loan move for highly-rated academy talent – report
description: Barcelona are beginning to accelerate several important squad decisions as preparations for the new season gather pace, and one of the club’s brightest academy prospects could soon be at the centre ...
fullText:
Barcelona are beginning to accelerate several important squad decisions as preparations for the new season gather pace, and one of the club’s brightest academy prospects could soon be at the centre of those plans.

With the 2026 FIFA World Cup now behind them, the Blaugrana have turned their full attention back to club football.

While much of the focus remains on first-team arrivals and departures, Barcelona are also assessing the development of some of La Masia’s most promising youngsters.

According to SPORT, left-back Jofre Torrents is among the players whose future is currently under review.

Although the teenager is highly regarded within the club, Barcelona are considering whether a loan move would provide the best platform for his continued development.

Jofre Torrents could leave Barcelona on loan. (Photo by Rafa Babot/Getty Images)

Torrents has been training with Hansi Flick’s first-team squad during pre-season, and rather than questioning his quality, the discussions revolve around ensuring the youngster receives regular competitive football.

Barcelona believe consistent minutes at senior level could accelerate his progress far more than remaining on the fringes of the first team.

That view has attracted attention from across Europe.

As reported before, clubs from Germany, the Netherlands, Portugal, France, Belgium, and Spain have all made enquiries about the left-back’s situation.

For now, Torrents’ representatives are evaluating the available options before any decision is taken regarding the next step in his career.

Jofre Torrents is rated very highly within Barcelona. (Photo by Angel Martinez/Getty Images)

Barcelona’s depth on the left side of defence is another key factor influencing the club’s thinking.

The Catalans are continuing to work on completing the permanent signing of Joao Cancelo, while Alejandro Balde is determined to establish himself under Flick after returning for pre-season.

If both players remain part of the squad, opportunities for Torrents would naturally become limited during the upcoming campaign.

Hence, a temporary move would allow the youngster to gain valuable first-team experience without closing the door on his long-term future at Camp Nou.

Importantly, Barcelona are not rushing into a decision, as the club remain convinced of Torrents’ ability and view any loan purely as part of his development rather than a step away from their long-term project.

title: Wilson can bring something 'very special' to Leeds - Ampadu
description: Leeds United captain Ethan Ampadu believes new signing Harry Wilson can bring something "very special" to the team through his contributions in attacking areas.  Ampadu and Wilson are team-mates at international level for Wales and the midfielder was asked by BBC Radio Leeds about whether he helped convince the attacker to make the switch from Fulham to Elland Road.  "I don't think he needed my convincing to join," Ampadu said.
fullText:
Leeds United captain Ethan Ampadu believes new signing Harry Wilson can bring something "very special" to the team through his contributions in attacking areas.

Ampadu and Wilson are team-mates at international level for Wales and the midfielder was asked by BBC Radio Leeds about whether he helped convince the attacker to make the switch from Fulham to Elland Road.

"I don't think he needed my convincing to join," Ampadu said. "I think he knew what an amazing club this is, what a great time he can have here. I like to think my message or two might've aided him in that direction. I think whenever I've spoken to him in the past the Welsh lads on camp have spoken glowingly about Leeds.

"All the conversations he's had with the manager and club have gone in the right direction. Because I know him quite well and I know him on the pitch, I'm grateful that we've got him in.

"I think everyone saw more last year, I think maybe because he was coming to the end of his contract that gets people talking about you in a different way but his quality, his left foot, the way he works for the team, the way he works for himself, I think all Leeds fans are going to appreciate the hard work he does and the goals and assists is very special and what helps results."

Ampadu also touched on how the Leeds team will have to change this coming season after achieving their goal of staying in the Premier League.

"I think you always have to try to adapt," he said. "You always have got to lean on what worked and you've also got to adapt on what you can improve, so I think any way we can do that is going to be done.

"I think collectively as a team we want to improve no matter which way it is, that's why we have pre-season. It's one of the most demanding leagues in the world, so we know the physicality has got to be there again. We've got to try and outrun teams again and make sure we're hard to play against and then things adapt because you want to try and find ways to win."

Listen to the full chat with Ampadu here

Find more Leeds content on BBC Sounds

title: Jeff Bezos ‘approached’ to join consortium in talks over Liverpool investment
description: Liverpool owners Fenway Sports Group have confirmed that a consortium led by former QPR co-owner Amit Bhatia is in talks over a minority investment
fullText:
Jeff Bezos, the Amazon founder and fourth-richest man in the world, has been “approached” by a consortium that is in talks to buy a minority stake in Liverpool, according to reports.

Liverpool owners Fenway Sports Group confirmed on Tuesday that they had been approached by a consortium led by Amit Bhatia, the British-Indian millionaire and former co-owner of QPR.

Sky News reported that Bezos has held “discussions” with the group, with talks still at an early stage.

Bezos, whose fortune is reportedly worth $257bn and who owns the Washington Post, explored bids for two NFL teams last year, the Seattle Seahawks and Washington Commanders, but did not proceed with either deal.

A spokesperson for FSG said on Tuesday: “An investment consortium led, managed, and represented by Amit Bhatia has expressed interest in making a strategic minority investment in Liverpool Football Club.”

Bhatia, the son-in-law of Lakshimi Mittal, the billionaire executive of steel production giant ArcelorMittal, also confirmed his departure from QPR’s board on Tuesday, the same day as FSG confirmed the talks with his consortium.

FSG sold a minority stake in Liverpool to investment firm Dynasty in 2023, which brought an injection of cash into the club following the pandemic, but did not fund future transfers.

title: Maeda in, Philogene out? Your Ipswich squad tweaks
description: Shaun: I would love to sign Zian Flemming from Burnley and I would offload George Hirst if possible.  Reuben: I would say (and some people might disagree with me) that Jack Clarke should leave the club, as long as Jaden Philogene stays.  James: Daizen Maeda from Celtic would be great.
fullText:
We asked you to pick one player you would like to move on this summer and one realistic target you want Ipswich to bring in before the transfer window shuts.

Here are some of your suggestions:

Shaun: I would love to sign Zian Flemming from Burnley and I would offload George Hirst if possible.

Reuben: I would say (and some people might disagree with me) that Jack Clarke should leave the club, as long as Jaden Philogene stays. I think we should cash in on a player who's had a good season but is really not good enough for the top division. He was practically non-existent in our last season in the Premier League and I still think he won't be good in the top flight. He has shown some great performances, but he has been sloppy in many areas of his game.

James: Daizen Maeda from Celtic would be great. He is an all-action forward, with goals and assists to his game. I would say a reluctant goodbye to Philogene. If he has designs on leaving. It isn't good to hold on to a player with those intentions.

John: I heard Ipswich are in for Maeda from Celtic. Do not hang about - this guy can run and run, and gives 100% no matter where he starts (but he is best out wide on the left).

George: We should go all out for Liam Delap. Bring him back on a season-long loan with an option to buy. It would suit all three parties, especially after a confidence-destroying spell at Chelsea.

title: What reception would Rashford get back at Man Utd?
description: Clearly just as United need active sellers to buy from, they need buyers so they can offload those players they do not want.  In Marcus Rashford's case, there are a limited number of clubs globally who could match his current salary.  BBC Sport has also been told of some Premier League clubs he would not be keen to move to.
fullText:
Clearly just as United need active sellers to buy from, they need buyers so they can offload those players they do not want.

In Marcus Rashford's case, there are a limited number of clubs globally who could match his current salary. And where a club are interested, the player has to want to join them.

Judging by his recent social media post, he would not do anything to jeopardise his England place.

BBC Sport has also been told of some Premier League clubs he would not be keen to move to.

So where will Rashford be when the transfer window closes on 1 September? Currently, the likeliest answer, by some distance, is Manchester United.

After that: Any Spanish club, any Turkish club, any German club. The vagueness there underlines the current lack of market for the 15th highest scorer in United's history, with 138 goals - someone who is only 17 short of a place in the top 10.

There are plenty, including former captain Gary Neville, who feel Rashford's time at Old Trafford is over and there should be no reconciliation.

In the end though, it may have to happen - and not everyone would be disappointed.

"A fit and focused Marcus Rashford has the ability to be one of the best players," said Rashford's friend Sam Smith - the Wrexham forward who scored the winner in their pre-season victory over Carrick's team in Helsinki on Saturday - when speaking to the United We Stand podcast.

"He has had a difficult time at United and getting away from it all was a good thing.

"I don't know what is going to happen but as his friend, I hope he comes back and proves how good a player he was, how good he can be and starts scoring goals again."

Read more from Simon on Rashford

title: 'Not against an update' but 'a badge isn't just a tool for merchandise'
description: Of course, fans will keep on coming and loving the club as much as they always did, but it breaks a bond, and only adds to the harmful emotional detachment between the modern game and the football fans' memories.  Ian: I'd rather the crest remained the same, but I accept the argument for changing it.
fullText:
We asked for your views after Newcastle announced they would allow eligible supporters to vote on new club crest options in August as work continues to update the existing badge.

Here are some of your comments:

Ed: A badge isn't just a tool for merchandise. Fans have so much emotional investment into a badge, a hat, scarf or a shirt which reminds you of that game, or that goal, or that person who you went to the game with. It's impossible to "evolve" aspects of the club which carry so much emotional weight without damaging one of the key bases of the fans' support. Of course, fans will keep on coming and loving the club as much as they always did, but it breaks a bond, and only adds to the harmful emotional detachment between the modern game and the football fans' memories.

Ian: I'd rather the crest remained the same, but I accept the argument for changing it. I think the crest vote should be open to all members and that there must be a "none of the above" option.

Matt: It's hard to say supporters are getting their say when the club came out and said this is happening whether anyone likes it or not. These new crests represent a simplified, homogenised version of everything that has ramped up post-Covid. I can already see the final product - a stripped-down version of a crest that is more caricature than representative of anything meaningful.

Phil: I'm not against an update and I like that the club at least seem to be listening to fans instead of barrelling into some soulless, corporate design. A modernisation of what we have wouldn't be the worst way to show prospective players/investors we're an ambitious and forward-thinking club. Stadium updates to follow suit, I hope!

Shawsy: The rationale behind the change is fair and understandable. Their original decision to garner initial thoughts from fans was laudable. They are modernising what is already there. This seems to be the right way forward and consulting with us along the way - that's good too. I'm excited to see what they come up with and having a say. Would be good to see the final result for the 2027-28 season - when we are back in the Champions League...

Andy: As much as I love the badge, its history, its identity, we have to understand that things change. Everything changes. Whatever brand you look at, there has been evolution to meet the demands of the modern world and we should be no different. It seems like they're trying to go about it in the right, respectful way, but we have to let it happen. Put together three options and let the fans vote - boom, done.

title: Lipowitz suffers broken collarbone in Tour crash as season at risk
description: Top German rider Florian Lipowitz broke his collarbone in a crash on the 16th stage of the Tour de France and his season could now be compromised. Last year's third-place finisher was fifth overall heading into Tuesday's time trial but a heavy crash at speed meant he had to withdraw from the race given his injury, which was confirmed by his Red Bull team.
fullText:
Top German rider Florian Lipowitz broke his collarbone in a crash on the 16th stage of the Tour de France and his season could now be compromised.

Last year's third-place finisher was fifth overall heading into Tuesday's time trial but a heavy crash at speed meant he had to withdraw from the race given his injury, which was confirmed by his Red Bull team.

He will miss at least six weeks, which makes the Vuelta (August 22 to September 13) impossible and the World Championships in Montreal (September 20 to 27) unlikely. The one-day races in Italy at the end of the season such as the Tour of Lombardy (October 10) are still doable.

It was a bitter-sweet day for the team given other leading rider Remco Evenepoel won the stage to trim Tadej Pogačar's overall lead to 4 minutes 32 seconds. The Tour concludes on Sunday.

Team boss Ralph Denk said: "We will not only miss Florian in the fight for the podium, we will also miss him here as a person. We won but it is not as if it is super great for us. I also feel sorry for Remco that not everything here is peace, joy and pancakes."

title: Las Vegas Raiders owner Mark Davis weighs in on grass fields movement after the World Cup
description: Las Vegas Raiders owner Mark Davis has broken his silence on the demand for NFL teams to use grass fields on the back of this summer’s World Cup. The Allegiant Stadium was not one of the host venues for the United States at the World Cup, but it is one of the NFL stadiums that uses a natural grass playing surface.
fullText:
Las Vegas Raiders owner Mark Davis has broken his silence on the demand for NFL teams to use grass fields on the back of this summer’s World Cup.

The Allegiant Stadium was not one of the host venues for the United States at the World Cup, but it is one of the NFL stadiums that uses a natural grass playing surface.

There is an argument to be made that a natural surface is easier for Vegas to maintain given it is an indoor venue, which does not expose the grass to the elements.

However, Davis is one of the owners who believes playing on a grass field is largely beneficial for his players’ health and wellbeing.

This is not a view that is shared across the league, with numerous NFL players now protesting their thoughts after seeing grass pitches laid down for the World Cup soccer teams.
